query: Never miss a Claude Code session waiting for your input complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Claude Input Notify: Never Miss a Claude Code Notification Again
results.content:
`claude-input-notify.sh`

## The Problem

When using Claude Code, there are moments when it pauses and waits for user input - asking for
confirmation, additional information, or next steps. If you’re working in another window or focused
on a different task, you might miss these notifications, leaving Claude waiting indefinitely and
disrupting your workflow.

## The Solution

The `claude-input-notify.sh` script uses Claude Code’s hooks system and automatically displays a
visual notification popup whenever Claude Code needs user input. This ensures
you never miss an important interaction point.

`claude-input-notify.sh`

Claude Input Notification Dialog

Claude Input Notification Dialog

## Key Features

### Smart Terminal Detection [...] ## Key Features

### Smart Terminal Detection

The script uses multiple methods to find your active terminal window:

### Visual Notification System

### Developer-Friendly Options

`DEBUG_MODE=1`
`TEST_MODE=1`

## Installation and Setup

`sudo apt install xdotool wmctrl yad jq`
`# Download the notification script
wget  -O ~/bin/claude-input-notify.sh
chmod +x ~/bin/claude-input-notify.sh
# Download the Claude logo (optional, but recommended for better visuals)
wget  -O ~/bin/claude-logo.png`
`~/.claude/settings.json`
`{
"hooks": {
"Notification": [
{
"hooks": [
{
"type": "command",
"command": "~/bin/claude-input-notify.sh"
}
]
}
]
}
}`

This hooks configuration ensures that the notification script runs automatically whenever Claude
Code needs user input - no manual piping required! [...] If you’re a frequent Claude Code user working in the terminal, give `claude-input-notify.sh` a try.
Combined with Claude’s hooks configuration, it provides a frictionless way to stay engaged with your
AI assistant, ensuring you never leave Claude waiting for your input again!

`claude-input-notify.sh`

results.title: Push Notifications for Claude Code: Never Miss a Prompt Again – Gianluca Nieri
results.content:
# Gianluca Nieri

# Push Notifications for Claude Code: Never Miss a Prompt Again

Published on 13 March 2026 · 100DaysToOffload, gen-ai

You step away from your desk to grab a coffee. When you come back, Claude Code has been waiting for your input for twelve minutes. The task is stalled and you just burned a chunk of your session staring at a coffee machine.

If you use Claude Code daily, you know this feeling.

## The problem

Claude Code runs in a terminal. When it needs your input, it waits. Silently.

Your terminal emulator (iTerm2, Ghostty, etc.) might show a system notification if you’re at your desk. But what about when you’re on another Mac across the room, on your phone on the couch, making lunch, or in a meeting with your laptop closed? [...] Add the `hooks` section to your existing settings:

```
{ "permissions": { "allow": [ "WebFetch", "Bash(curl )", "Read" ] }, "hooks": { "Notification": [ { "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "bash ~/.claude/notify-hook.sh" } ] } ] } }
```

Claude Code’s hook system supports several event types. The `Notification` event fires whenever Claude Code would normally show a system notification, typically when it’s waiting for user input after completing a step.

Each event type contains an array of hook groups, and each group has a `hooks` array with the actual commands. `type: "command"` tells Claude Code to run a shell command.

### 4. The shell alias

Add to `~/.zshrc`:

```
alias claude-notify='bash ~/.claude/claude-notify.sh'
``` [...] ## Usage

A typical session:

```
# Start of your work session claude-notify on # -> Notifiche push Claude Code: ATTIVE # Check status claude-notify status # -> Notifiche push Claude Code: ATTIVE # Work normally claude # Whenever Claude Code needs your input, your phone buzzes # End of session (or just leave them on) claude-notify off # -> Notifiche push Claude Code: DISATTIVATE
```

## Verification

To test the whole chain:

results.title: Claude Code Notifier - Never Miss a Prompt
results.content:
## Stop Losing Hours to Stalled Sessions

You know the feeling. You kick off Claude on a complex refactor, step away for coffee, and return twenty minutes later to discover it asked a clarifying question in the first minute—and has been waiting ever since.

That's twenty minutes of productivity gone. Multiply that across a day of coding, and you've lost hours.

Stalled Claude Code session

## Track All Your Sessions

Running Claude in three different terminal windows across two monitors? No problem. Claude Code Notifier tracks all your active sessions and tells you exactly which one needs you.

No more cycling through tabs wondering where that notification sound came from. Every session is tracked independently.

Multiple terminal support

## Smart Notifications That Don't Annoy [...] Claude Code Notifier

# Never Miss a Prompt. Never Lose Momentum.

The essential companion app for Claude Code that keeps you productive whether you're at your desk or on the go. Get instant notifications when Claude needs your input.

Claude Code Notifier in action

## Mobile Companion Apps

Step away from your desk without losing momentum. Get instant push notifications on your phone when Claude needs your attention—whether you're in a meeting, grabbing coffee, or working from the couch.

Mobile companion apps showing Claude Code notifications

## Stop Losing Hours to Stalled Sessions [...] ### Download

Download and install Claude Code Notifier from our website

### Grant Permissions

Allow notification permissions when prompted by macOS

### Install Hook

Click "Install Hook" in the menu bar app

### You're Protected

That's it. Never miss a prompt again

## Everything You Need

Built for developers who demand the best tools for their workflow.

#### Session Analytics

Track your Claude Code usage over time. See how many sessions you run, how many prompts you respond to, and identify patterns in your workflow.

#### Privacy First

All data stays on your device. No accounts, no cloud sync, no telemetry. Your code is your business.

#### Lightweight

The hook adds negligible overhead—it's a simple event emission that runs asynchronously. You won't notice it's there.

## Problem

When running multiple Claude Code sessions simultaneously (e.g., across different projects or tasks), there's no way to know which session is blocked waiting for user input — permission approvals, questions, etc. You have to manually check each tab/window.

## Proposed Solution

A built-in notification mechanism when Claude Code is waiting for user input. This could be: [...] Skip to content

# Notification when Claude Code is waiting for user input #36885

Copy link

Copy link

Closed as not planned

Closed as not planned [...] ## Use Cases

 Running 3-4 Claude Code sessions across different projects
 Headless Mac mini setup accessed via phone/SSH — need ambient audio feedback
 Long-running autonomous tasks where Claude occasionally needs a permission grant
 Pair programming flow where you're focused elsewhere and need to know when Claude has a question

## Why This Matters

Claude Code is moving toward more autonomous, multi-session workflows. The bottleneck increasingly isn't Claude's speed — it's the human noticing that Claude is waiting. A simple notification closes that gap.

## Description

@lowellk

lowellk

opened on Jan 9, 2026

Issue body actions

## Description

Getting false macOS notifications saying "Claude is waiting for your input" when Claude has already finished responding and is idle.

## Screenshot

The notification appears in the top-right even though Claude Code has completed its response and is showing the prompt waiting for user input (not waiting for anything from the user in terms of a tool approval or question).

Image

## Environment

 Claude Code version: 2.1.1
 macOS (Darwin 25.2.0)
 Terminal: iTerm2
 Model: Opus 4.5 [...] Skip to content

# False 'waiting for input' notifications on macOS #16975

Copy link

Copy link

Closed as duplicate

Closed as duplicate [...] ## Expected behavior

Notifications should only fire when Claude is genuinely waiting for user input (e.g., tool approval, answering a question from Claude).

## Actual behavior

Notification fires even after Claude has completed its response and the session is idle.
